Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} 鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=諏訪神社 | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}下米田町今元入会地5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|建御名方神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=4月第1土・日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=諏訪神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|美濃加茂市|Q857305}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の中山神社の論社である（もう一つの論社は{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}）。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。元々は美濃国賀茂郡下飯田村中山（現・{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|川辺町|Q1203748}}下飯田）に鎮座していたという。別説では現在の{{jalink|中山神社 (美濃加茂市)|Q11364249|中山神社}}の位置が鎮座地という。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間に兵火で焼失する。その後{{jalink|米田城}}城主{{jalink|肥田玄蕃}}の命で肥田長寿丸が現在地に再建する。この際、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}を祀り、諏訪神社に改称する。 {{jalink|1981年|Q2437}}（昭和56年）4月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金84号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=65}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|建御名方神}} === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|中川辺駅|Q5358732}}より約1.5km。 === 例祭 === *毎年4月第1土・日曜日に行なわれる。 :{{jalink|武田信玄|Q276404}}と{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}の像を乗せた2台の{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}が参道を引き回される。斜面を登り神社に到着すると、米田囃子が演奏される。このだんじりの引き回しのさい、これを見物している女性を追い掛け回す{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866|獅子}}を、天狗の面をして{{jalink|サカキ|Q775470|榊}}を手にした拝翁がそれを戒めるということが行なわれる。 :奉納{{jalink|相撲|Q40561}}が行われる。 === 文化財 === * 大杉（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=指定文化財 |publisher=美濃加茂市 |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} / {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Minokamo|Q857305}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656|Kamo District}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{jalink|Kawabe|Q1203748}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{jalink|Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{jalink|Yoneda Castle}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} of {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{jalink|1981|Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Kami}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{jalink|Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{jalink|Takeda Shingen|Q276404}} and {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866|lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{jalink|sakaki|Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{jalink|sumo|Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill||lt=Yoneda Castle|UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill||lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039907}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
