== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや みのかも}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSuwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)}}[[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006496 == Commons category (P373) == * Suwa-jinja (Minokamo) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakayamano Shrine|lt=Nakayamano Shrine|WD=Q135039907|1=Nakayamano Shrine (Q135039907)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県美濃加茂市下米田町今元入会地5', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suwa-jinja (Minokamo)}} [[JA:諏訪神社 (美濃加茂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11631934}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:Suwa-jinja, Minokamo.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} Shimo-Yoneda-chō, Imamoto Nyūaichi 5 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=first Saturday/Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Suwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minokamo|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for Nakayama Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|lt=Kamo District|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (the other candidate is {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}). === History === The founding period is unknown. It is said to have originally stood at Nakayama in Shimo-Iida Village, Kamo District, Mino Province (present Shimo-Iida, {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}). Another theory says the location was that of current {{ill|lt=Nakayama Shrine (Minokamo)|Q11364249|WD=Q11364249|lt=Nakayama Shrine|1=Nakayama Shrine (Q11364249)}}. It burned down in war during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era. Later, by order of Hida Genba, lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoneda Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hida Chōjumaru rebuilt it at the present site. At that time, {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} was enshrined and the name was changed to Suwa Shrine. On April 1, {{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|WD=Q2437|1=1981}} (Shōwa 56), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}, designation No. Kin-84){{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=65}}. === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} === Transportation === *About 1.5 km from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. === Annual festival === *Held every year on the first Saturday and Sunday of April. :Two {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} carrying statues of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} and {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} are pulled through the approach. When they climb the slope and reach the shrine, Yoneda-bayashi music is played. During the procession, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} chases women watching, and a haiō figure wearing a tengu mask and holding {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches restrains it. :A dedicatory {{ill|lt=sumo|Q40561|WD=Q40561|1=Sumo}} event is held. === Cultural properties === * Great cedar (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.minokamo.gifu.jp/syoukai/toukei/H11_TOUKEI/16/16_23.html |title=Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Minokamo City |accessdate=2013-04-20}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suwa Shrine (Q11631934)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:美濃加茂市の建築物]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]]
